Here are some GitHub repositories related to machine learning:

1. **[josephmisiti/awesome-machine-learning](https://github.com/josephmisiti/awesome-machine-learning)**  
   A curated list of awesome Machine Learning frameworks, libraries, and software.  
   - Stars: 72,661  
   - Language: Python  
   - Last updated: June 2, 2026  

2. **[wepe/MachineLearning](https://github.com/wepe/MachineLearning)**  
   Basic Machine Learning and Deep Learning.  
   - Stars: 5,699  
   - Language: Python  
   - Last updated: June 2, 2026  

3. **[Jack-Cherish/Machine-Learning](https://github.com/Jack-Cherish/Machine-Learning)**  
   实战（Python3）：kNN、决策树、贝叶斯、逻辑回归、SVM、线性回归、树回归.  
   - Stars: 10,323  
   - Language: Python  
   - Last updated: June 2, 2026  

4. **[lawlite19/MachineLearning_Python](https://github.com/lawlite19/MachineLearning_Python)**  
   机器学习算法python实现.  
   - Stars: 8,501  
   - Language: Python  
   - Last updated: June 2, 2026  

5. **[udacity/machine-learning](https://github.com/udacity/machine-learning)**  
   Content for Udacity's Machine Learning curriculum.  
   - Stars: 4,027  
   - Language: Jupyter Notebook  
   - Last updated: June 1, 2026  

Feel free to explore these repositories for more information!

turns.user: tóm tắt về Transformer trên wiki
turns.assistant_text:
The Transformer is a family of artificial neural network architectures used in deep learning, primarily based on the multi-head attention mechanism. In this architecture, text is converted into numerical representations called tokens, which are then transformed into vectors using a word embedding table.

Key features of the Transformer include:

- **Contextualization**: Each token is contextualized within a context window alongside other (unmasked) tokens through a parallel multi-head attention mechanism. This allows important tokens to be amplified while less significant ones are diminished.
- **Positional Information**: Since self-attention is permutation-invariant, Transformers incorporate positional information, typically through positional encodings or learned positional embeddings, enabling the order of tokens to influence the output.

For more detailed information, you can visit the [Wikipedia page on Transformers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transformer_(deep_learning)).

items.title: Transformer (deep learning)
items.summary: In deep learning, the transformer is a family of artificial neural network architectures based on the multi-head attention mechanism, in which text is converted to numerical representations called tokens, and each token is converted into a vector via lookup from a word embedding table. At each layer, each token is then contextualized within the scope of the context window with other (unmasked) tokens via a parallel multi-head attention mechanism, allowing the signal for key tokens to be amplified and less important tokens to be diminished. Because self-attention alone is permutation-invariant, transformers inject positional information, typically through positional encodings or learned positional embeddings, so token order can affect the output.

items.title: Transformer (deep learning)
items.summary: In deep learning, the transformer is a family of artificial neural network architectures based on the multi-head attention mechanism, in which text is converted to numerical representations called tokens, and each token is converted into a vector via lookup from a word embedding table. At each layer, each token is then contextualized within the scope of the context window with other (unmasked) tokens via a parallel multi-head attention mechanism, allowing the signal for key tokens to be amplified and less important tokens to be diminished. Because self-attention alone is permutation-invariant, transformers inject positional information, typically through positional encodings or learned positional embeddings, so token order can affect the output.
